sections.label: THINKING
sections.content:
Good progress! Square moved from (47, 29) to (52, 27), magenta from (49, 30) to (54, 28). G is at (57, 27).
The square is now at (52, 27) and G is at (57, 27). They're on the same column! The square just needs to move down 5 more rows.
But wait, the square boundary from before showed it spans rows 54-59 when it was at (54, 26). So the square is 6 rows tall (roughly). If the square is now at (52, 27), it probably extends to about row 57-58, which means it's already touching G at (57, 27)!
Let me check if clicking magenta once more will complete the level or if the square is already touching G.
Let me try clicking magenta once more.

sections.label: ASSISTANT
sections.content:
World model (revised):
- Square at (52, 27), magenta at (54, 28), G at (57, 27)
- Square and G are on same column, square may be touching G
Goal model: Click magenta once more to complete the level
Plan: Click magenta at (54, 28)

sections.label: TOOL CALL: python
sections.content:
<tool_call>
<function=python>
<parameter=code>
# Click magenta to advance square onto G
predict(expect_change=True, note='Click magenta to reach G')
action([{'action': 'MOUSE', 'row': 54, 'col': 28}])
print(f"Result: level_completed={last_action_result.get('level_completed')}, done={last_action_result.get('done')}, game_over={last_action_result.get('game_over')}")

seg = current_frame.segmentation
print("=== After click ===")
for n in seg['nodes']:
    if n['color'] in ('M', 'W', 'G', 'g', 'p') and n['pixels'] < 50:
        r, c = n['boundary'][0]
        print(f"{n['color']}: pixels={n['pixels']}, at ({r}, {c})")
</parameter>
</function>
</tool_call>
